content:
# Revelation Chapter 13 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (a), 4th century CE, pages 286-287 | **Source Image:** REV_013_page_001.jpg, REV_013_page_002.jpg

1. And I saw a beast rising out of the sea, having ten horns and seven heads, and upon his horns ten diadems, and upon his heads names of blasphemy.

2. And the beast that I saw was like a leopard, and his feet were like those of a bear, and his mouth like the mouth of a lion. And the dragon gave him his power and his throne and great authority.

3. And one of his heads was as though slain unto death, and his wound of death was healed. And the whole earth marveled after the beast,

4. and they worshiped the dragon, because he gave authority to the beast, and they worshiped the beast, saying, "Who is like the beast, and who is able to wage war with him?"

5. And there was given to him a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies, and there was given to him authority to act for forty-two months.

6. And he opened his mouth in blasphemies against God, to blaspheme his name and his tabernacle, those who dwell in heaven.

7. And it was given to him to make war with the saints and to overcome them, and there was given to him authority over every tribe and people and tongue and nation.

8. And all who dwell upon the earth will worship him, everyone whose name has not been written in the book of life of the Lamb who has been slain from the foundation of the world.

9. If anyone has an ear, let him hear.

10. If anyone is destined for captivity, into captivity he goes. If anyone is to be killed with the sword, with the sword he must be killed. Here is the endurance and the faith of the saints.

11. And I saw another beast rising out of the earth, and he had two horns like a lamb, and he spoke like a dragon.

12. And he exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence, and he makes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose wound of death was healed.

13. And he performs great signs, so that he even makes fire come down from heaven to the earth in the presence of men.

14. And he deceives those who dwell upon the earth through the signs which were given him to perform in the presence of the beast, telling those who dwell upon the earth to make an image to the beast who has the wound of the sword and yet lived.

15. And it was given to him to give breath to the image of the beast, so that the image of the beast should even speak, and cause as many as would not worship the image of the beast to be killed.

16. And he causes all, the small and the great, and the rich and the poor, and the free and the slaves, to be given a mark on their right hand or on their forehead,

17. and that no one should be able to buy or to sell except the one who has the mark, the name of the beast or the number of his name.

18. Here is wisdom. Let him who has understanding calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man, and his number is six hundred sixty-six.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

- **13:1 — "names of blasphemy"**: ΟΝΟΜΑΤΑ ΒΛΑΣΦΗΜΙΑΣ — Sinaiticus reads the plural "names" (onomata) on the heads, whereas some later manuscripts read the singular "name." The plural is retained as it matches the multiple heads.
- **13:1 — "ten diadems"**: Sinaiticus places the diadems (ΔΙΑΔΗΜΑΤΑ) on the horns, not the heads (contrast the dragon in 12:3 where diadems are on the heads). This distinction is preserved.
- **13:2 — Danielic composite beast**: The leopard, bear, and lion echo Daniel 7:3-6 (LXX), but in reverse order. Daniel's four separate beasts are here combined into one, suggesting a culmination of all prior empires.
- **13:3 — "his wound of death" (hē plēgē tou thanatou)**: Η ΠΛΗΓΗ ΤΟΥ ΘΑΝΑΤΟΥ — literally "the plague/blow of death." Rendered "wound of death" for clarity. This phrase is a parody of the Lamb's death and resurrection (5:6).
- **13:6 — "his tabernacle, those who dwell in heaven"**: The phrase ΤΟΥΣ ΕΝ ΤΩ ΟΥΡΑΝΩ ΣΚΗΝΟΥΝΤΑΣ is appositional — "his tabernacle" is identified with "those who dwell in heaven." The tabernacle of God is his people.
- **13:8 — "from the foundation of the world"**: ΑΠΟ ΚΑΤΑΒΟΛΗΣ ΚΟΣΜΟΥ — this phrase may modify "slain" (the Lamb slain from the foundation) or "written" (names written from the foundation). The Greek word order in Sinaiticus favors attachment to "slain," and this reading is preserved.
- **13:10 — captivity saying**: Sinaiticus reads ΕΙ ΤΙΣ ΕΙΣ ΑΙΧΜΑΛΩΣΙΑΝ, ΕΙΣ ΑΙΧΜΑΛΩΣΙΑΝ ΥΠΑΓΕΙ — a chiastic structure echoing Jeremiah 15:2 and 43:11 (LXX). The saying calls for endurance under persecution, not active resistance.
- **13:11 — "like a lamb"**: ΟΜΟΙΑ ΑΡΝΙΩ — the same word (arnion) used throughout Revelation for Christ. The second beast parodies the Lamb while speaking with the dragon's voice.
- **13:18 — "six hundred sixty-six"**: ΕΞΑΚΟΣΙΟΙ ΕΞΗΚΟΝΤΑ ΕΞ — Sinaiticus reads 666 (χξϛ). Some manuscripts (notably P115 and C) read 616. Gematria calculations have produced many proposed identifications; the text itself simply calls for wisdom in discernment.

— LOGOS
